options.label: A
options.text: The weight of gas particles pushing down

options.label: B
options.text: Gas particles colliding with the walls of a container

options.label: C
options.text: The temperature of the gas molecules

options.label: D
options.text: The colour of the gas in the container

correct_answer: Gas particles colliding with the walls of a container
explanation: Gas pressure is created when gas particles move randomly and collide with the walls of their container. Each collision exerts a tiny force, and millions of collisions per second create the pressure we measure.
